transcript.clean_text: first day of road racing proper on lavuelta destination burgos the venue for the time trial which primos rockledge of course won he's starting off in the red jersey and the course today a zig zagging one that posed a few worries for everybody wind was the issue would it blow up and to what impact all three riders decided that they were going to take on the day as part of the breakaway and naturally enough it had to be with our destination being bergos that burgos bh were here with rubio martin was there for kaha roral and likewise as barin from oscar del escodi well not too many dramas despite some nerves being displayed at every twist and turn on this zigzag course and in the end our breakaway was starting to fall apart last man standing would be rubio from burgos keeping his fluids and indeed energy where they needed to be and energy something being displayed by astana they were working for the green jersey of arumburu nursing it on behalf of roglic wanted to make it his own he went for it on the intermediate sprint they went a little bit too early astana and were mugged in the end by fabio jacobson who happened to be in a good position and thought well why not so jakobson took the intermediate but could he take the big one at the end of the day heading for burgos well real estate was uh very expensive out there and at what cost riders crammed together the peloton almost as wide as it was long and it was borrowed that were the big big losers on the crash outside of the three kilometer zone or picking themselves up but with a very late finish meanwhile out front it was the final couple of corners that were starting to fill the mines of the sprinters how would it pan out with a diminished finale well the radio been very busy and jakobson himself said that communications were difficult and he lost his lead out train process azure and anna damar were also boxed in getting into trouble with 82r citroen on the the lead-in the team with the clearest vision mattia trentin set off for ua team emirates to guide milano into a good position he looked good for it as well michael matthews was also producing a strong performance only way round those two was left and right jacobson went one way phillips and went the other and in the end it was alpes and phoenix that got their victory phillips and absolutely delighted he went for the barriers on the left-hand side jakobson went right michael matthews bowing his head but a great performance by the australian to finish in third place well this victory enough to take the green jersey for jasper phillipson aaron burrow finished fifth on the day he's equal on points but the victory means that phillipson will wear green tomorrow primus roglic finishing safe within the pack as many of the main contenders as well he stays in red as we head for the big peak tomorrow the first of the climbs and he leads around by a margin of four seconds closing that gap with the bonus seconds he picked up on the intermediate sprint so how will it pan out tomorrow it's a long day 202 kilometers in total reasonably flat but plenty of climbing points up for grabs because it is an uphill category one test at the end the pikon blanco the white spike waits to impale somebody how will it run it'll be dramatic it's the welter for goodness sake can't wait for stage three you
